summary: Blazing Scarlet>Fire segue, tight set, Brent's standout keyboards
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition and an inspired second‑set flow. Brent Mydland’s analog jams add extra texture while Jerry’s guitar stays hot throughout. Overall energy is high and the setlist highlights several deep cuts that hold up well.
review:
April 13, 1983 at Patrick Field House in Burlington captured a quintessential early‑80s Dead night that still feels fresh. The first set rolls through classic material—Jack Straw, West L.A. Fadeaway, Far From Me—and even the occasional quirky banter from Bob keeps the vibe loose. The second set is where the magic truly ignites: a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue launches into a soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World stretch that culminates in an emotional Morning Dew. Brent Mydland’s keyboard work shines, especially during the transition, adding a warm analog groove that many fans cite as among his best for this era. Jerry Garcia’s lead guitar is consistently praised—blazing, inventive, and at times “smoking”—even when some mixes pull his tone low. Phil Lesh provides a solid bass foundation throughout, while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lock in a steady dual‑drum pulse. The intimate gym setting amplifies the energy, making this show a must‑listen for anyone hunting a vibrant snapshot of the 1983 tour.

For listeners seeking the clearest window into that night, the SBD capture by sacks (gd83-04-13.sbd.sacks.1614.sbeok.shnf) offers crisp balance with only minor volume quirks and an audience splice, delivering the most reliable listening experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five of six recordings, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Brent's keyboard jams, and overall high‑energy performance; mixed notes are limited to a few weak songs but no consistent negative criticism.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with balanced mix, minimal technical issues, and the most complete representation of the show’s energy.

key_highlights: Monumental Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue
Brent Mydland's analog keyboard jams add texture throughout
Soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World → Morning Dew sequence
High‑energy gym atmosphere that fuels the entire performance

band_performance.Jerry: Blazing, inventive guitar work that stays hot all night; magical solos described as ‘smoking’ and close‑up in audience tapes.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation, noted as ‘blazing’ alongside Jerry; occasional mix issues keep it a bit buried but rhythmically tight.
band_performance.Bob: Quirky spoken moments and funny banter add character; vocal delivery solid despite occasional horse‑like description.
band_performance.Brent: Analog keyboard jams stand out as some of his best for this period, especially during the Scarlet>Fire transition, adding groovy texture to the set.
